content.full_text:
SUPERSEDED
by Campaign forms
adopted by the Commission
on August 6, 1999, (Mensages
eliminated PartII of the
Allocation page.)

June 18, 1997


James R. Sutton
Nielsen, Merksamer, Parrinello,
Mueller & Naylor
591 Redwood Highway, #4000
Mill Valley, California 94941

Re:    Your Request for Informal Assistance
       Our File No. I-97-259

Dear Mr. Sutton:

       You have requested written confirmation of telephone advice provided to your office
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ Because your question is a
general inquiry, we are treating your letter as a request for informal assistance pursuant to
Regulation 18329(c)(3).²

QUESTION

       Do candidates who make campaign contributions to other candidates or ballot measures
from their personal funds have to file a Major Donor Committee Campaign Statement (Form
461)?

CONCLUSION

       Officeholders and candidates who make monetary and non-monetary contributions, loans,
and independent expenditures out of personal funds (including those made by a spouse from
jointly held funds) to support or oppose other officeholders, candidates, or committees must


¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, California Code of
Regulations Section 18000, et seq.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

Our File No. I-97-259
Page 2

report them on Part II of the Allocation Page of the Officeholder, Candidate, and Controlled
Committee Campaign Statement - Long Form (Form 490). Such contributions and expenditures
are not required to be reported on any other schedule. Therefore, candidates who make campaign
contributions to other candidates or ballot measures from their personal funds have no
requirement to file a Major Donor Committee Campaign Statement (Form 461). (Reynolds
Advice Letter, No. A-82-213.)

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel


By: Trish Mayer
Political Reform Consultant
